title: .container unit always pulls image when referencing .image unit, even if image exists locally
body:
### Issue Description

When specifying an image in a .container unit using a reference to a .image unit (e.g., Image=test-pull-missing.image), the container runtime always attempts to pull the image, even if it is already present locally

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Define a .image unit for a private registry image (e.g., test-pull-missing.image).
2. Reference this unit in a .container unit using Image=test-pull-missing.image.
3. Ensure the image is already present locally.
4. Start the container unit.


test-pull-missing.image
```
[Unit]
Description=Test Pull Missing Image

[Image]
Image=registry/test-pull-missing:1.0
AuthFile=/etc/containers/systemd/authfile.json
```

test-pull-missing.container
```
[Unit]
Description=Test Pull missing container

[Container]
ContainerName=test-pull-missing

# This image specification works fine with the Pull policy:
# Image=registry/test-pull-missing:1.0

# This one does not work:
Image=test-pull-missing.image

Pull=missing
```

### Describe the results you received

```
Jun  8 09:59:24 host-container test-pull-missing-image[8377]: Error: initializing source docker://registry/test-pull-missing:1.0: pinging container registry registry: Get "https://registry/v2/": dial tcp 127.0.0.1:443: connect: connection refused
```
Despite the image being available locally, the system attempts to reach the registry and fails due to custom authentication and no network access.


### Describe the results you expected

If the image is already present locally, it should not be pulled again.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: I think the main point is that a image unit is resolved to podman pull and that command tries to pull a new image even if it already exists which is generally the right behavior

The primary purpose of using the .image unit in my case is to enable authentication for accessing container images.

However, in this scenario, if the container registry becomes unavailable or the image has been deleted, the .container service — even with preloaded images — becomes completely non-functional after a restart.

If authentication is not used and the image is specified directly in the .container unit, everything works as intended when Pull=missing is set.

yes I get that, just saying the reason pull tries to pull is expected since that is basically Pull=always

Practically speaking podman run supports `--authfile` as well so technically we could just as well add it to the container unit.

Or we might have add the pull policy to podman pull command as well so you could set it to missing. Or switch the quadlet generator to add `podman image exists $IMAGE || podman pull $IMAGE` as command so we only run pull if it does not exists already.

cc @ygalblum

Looking into the code, I found that the pull policy is a field in `ImagePullOptions` passed to `ImageEngine().Pull`. This method is called both by `podman run` and by `podman pull`. It's just that the latter does not support the flag and therefore does not send anything.
So, the adding this argument to `podman pull` seems pretty strait forward
I've created a PR for the pull command: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/26478. Once it's merged, we can add a key to Quadlet `.image` units
